insight:
Yesterday was a solid training day with your 9.5km run at a well-controlled 147 bpm average heart rate - that's perfect aerobic base building with just 16 days to Chester! Your 15,980 steps and 3,108 calories burned show great activity levels, plus that 43 bpm resting heart rate confirms you're in excellent cardiovascular shape.

However, that 5.3 hours of sleep is concerning, especially with your HRV elevated to 114ms (above your 90-112 baseline). While high HRV can indicate good recovery, combined with inadequate sleep it often signals your nervous system is still processing yesterday's training load. I'd recommend keeping today's session light - maybe an easy 30-40 minute recovery run or cross-training.

Your biggest priority today is catching up on sleep debt from last night. Aim for a 20-minute power nap if possible, and focus on hitting 2,300 calories with quality protein to support recovery. Your body will thank you for it, and you'll be stronger for tomorrow's training.
